title: Instruct-Particulate: Scaling Feed-Forward 3D Object Articulation with Kinematic Control
headline: Instruct-Particulate: Scaling 3D Object Articulation with Kinematic Control via Instructional Models
tldr: We present Instruct-Particulate, a model that reconstructs articulated 3D objects from meshes and target kinematic specifications. By leveraging instruction-based conditioning, it generalizes across diverse object categories with limited annotated data.
significance: This approach enables scalable, data‑efficient reconstruction of articulated 3D models for animation, gaming, and robotics.

title: Leptonic flavour transfer: a new window on flavour gauge symmetries
headline: New Leptonic Flavour Transfer Mechanisms Reveal Hidden Gauge Symmetries in Particle Physics
tldr: The study introduces novel non‑Abelian flavour gauge groups that induce flavour‑transfer processes resembling charged‑current interactions in the lepton sector. By exploring various flavour structures, the authors systematically identify observable signatures that could probe these hidden symmetries at low energies.
significance: This work opens a new avenue for testing flavour gauge symmetries through accessible low‑energy lepton processes, potentially guiding future experimental searches.

title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
headline: Analogical Retrieval Enhances Complex Reasoning in Large Language Models' Performance
tldr: The paper introduces a retrieval-augmented reinforcement fine-tuning method that selects analogical examples to guide reasoning. Experiments show improved performance on reasoning benchmarks compared to standard lexical retrieval.
significance: It demonstrates that analogy-based retrieval can substantially improve reasoning in LLMs, offering a new direction for knowledge grounding.

title: Combined Analysis of Lattice QCD and Experimental Data on the Pion Transition Form Factor
headline: Combining Lattice QCD and Experimental Data to Refine the Pion Transition Form Factor
tldr: This study demonstrates a method to merge lattice QCD calculations with experimental measurements, enhancing the precision of the pion transition form factor. The approach leverages complementary strengths of both datasets to improve predictions for the muon g‑2 hadronic light‑by‑light contribution.
significance: Accurate determination of the pion transition form factor is essential for reducing uncertainties in the muon anomalous magnetic moment, a key test of the Standard Model.

title: Latent Spatial Memory for Video World Models
headline: Efficient Latent Spatial Memory Enhances 3D Consistency in Video World Models
tldr: This paper proposes a latent spatial memory that replaces explicit RGB point clouds, reducing computational cost and preserving richer latent features. The approach maintains 3D spatial consistency across generated frames without repeated rendering or VAE encoding.
significance: By eliminating costly rendering loops, the method enables scalable, high-fidelity video generation with robust 3D coherence.

title: OmniGameArena: A Unified UE5 Benchmark for VLM Game Agents with Improvement Dynamics
headline: OmniGameArena: Unified Benchmark for Vision‑Language Game Agents with Dynamic Evaluation
tldr: OmniGameArena introduces a single protocol that evaluates commercial, open‑weight, and specialized VLM agents across multiple games. It reports progressive improvement metrics instead of single‑attempt scores, enabling fair comparison of heterogeneous agent classes.
significance: This benchmark standardizes evaluation, enabling researchers to compare diverse VLM agents on equal footing and track learning progress in interactive game environments.

title: TSseek: Regular Expression-Based Similarity Search for Distributed Time Series Datasets
headline: TSseek: Regex-Based Pattern Search for Distributed Time Series Data in Big Data
tldr: TSseek introduces a regex-based similarity search engine that allows users to query distributed time series datasets using flexible pattern expressions instead of full sequences. The system supports scalable, distributed processing and demonstrates significant speedups over traditional exact-match methods.
significance: By enabling pattern-based queries, TSseek expands the applicability of time series analysis to real-world scenarios where precise sequences are unavailable.

title: Causally Evaluating the Learnability of Formal Language Tasks
headline: Measuring How Much Data Language Models Need to Master Formal Tasks
tldr: This study introduces a causal framework to quantify the amount of task-specific data required for language models to learn formal language tasks. By using controlled experiments, it demonstrates that data frequency strongly predicts learnability, offering a benchmark for future multi-task learning research.
significance: It provides a rigorous, data-driven benchmark for assessing and improving the efficiency of large language models across diverse tasks.

title: Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL
headline: Rethinking Divergence Regularization for Stable RL in Large Language Models
tldr: This paper revisits divergence regularization in RL for LLMs, highlighting issues with ratio-clipping in PPO and GRPO. It proposes a more principled trust-region approach that improves stability and performance.
significance: It offers a new perspective on stabilizing off-policy RL for LLMs, potentially enhancing alignment and safety.

title: Linear Ricci-Trace Deformations and Operational Equivalence in Rastall-Type Gravity
headline: Exploring Linear Ricci-Trace Deformations in Rastall-Type Gravity: Structural Classification and Parameter Mapping
tldr: We classify linear Ricci–trace deformations of Einstein’s equations, revealing a family of Rastall-type models with modified Ricci-to-scalar coupling. The analysis provides a parameter dictionary linking these deformations to observable gravitational dynamics.
significance: This work clarifies the theoretical landscape of modified gravity, offering a systematic framework for future phenomenological studies.

title: Satellite compaction pathways: environmental drivers shaping dwarf galaxy corpulence in the TNG50 simulation
headline: Environmental Drivers of Compact Dwarf Galaxies in the TNG50 Simulation
tldr: We investigate how environmental factors lead to compact dwarf galaxies in the TNG50 simulation, focusing on satellite compaction pathways. Our analysis reveals that tidal interactions and gas stripping are key drivers of dwarf galaxy corpulence.
significance: This study provides a comprehensive framework for understanding the physical processes that produce compact dwarf satellites, informing models of galaxy evolution.

title: PTL-Diffusion: Manifold-Aware Diffusion with Periodic Terminal Laws
headline: PTL-Diffusion: Enhancing Diffusion Models with Manifold-Aware Periodic Terminal Laws
tldr: PTL-Diffusion introduces a manifold-aware diffusion framework that replaces the standard Gaussian terminal distribution with periodic terminal laws, enabling better modeling of data concentrated near low-dimensional manifolds. This approach improves generation quality for complex, structured datasets while maintaining analytical tractability.
significance: By aligning diffusion dynamics with underlying manifold structures, PTL-Diffusion offers a principled way to generate high-fidelity samples for data with rich geometric or semantic variations.

title: iMaC: Translating Actions into Motion and Contact Images for Embodied World Models
headline: iMaC: Bridging Actions to Motion Images for Robust Embodied AI
tldr: iMaC introduces a novel framework that translates low-dimensional action vectors into rich motion and contact images, enabling embodied world models to learn from visual dynamics rather than abstract parameters. The method demonstrates improved generalization across diverse robot embodiments and provides a benchmark dataset for future research.
significance: By converting actions into visual representations, iMaC unlocks more expressive, transferable, and physically grounded learning for embodied AI systems.

title: Non-Abelian braiding in Abelian Fractional Quantum Hall Phases from realistic interactions
headline: Realizing Non-Abelian Braiding in a Laughlin 1/3 Quantum Hall State
tldr: The study demonstrates that realistic two‑body interactions within the lowest Landau level can produce non‑Abelian braiding of quasiholes in the ν=1/3 Laughlin phase. Low‑energy excitations are found to lie almost entirely in the null space of the Moore‑Read three‑body Hamiltonian.
significance: It bridges the gap between Abelian and non‑Abelian quantum Hall physics, offering a realistic route to topological quantum computation.

title: Counterintuitive problems in discrete probability
headline: Counterintuitive Discrete Probability Problems and Solutions for LLM Benchmarking and Analysis of Common Errors
tldr: This manuscript presents a curated set of counterintuitive discrete probability puzzles, each accompanied by detailed solutions. The dataset serves as a benchmark to evaluate how well large language models reason through probabilistic problems and to identify systematic errors they may make.
significance: It provides a rigorous testbed for assessing and improving the probabilistic reasoning capabilities of modern LLMs.

title: How reliable are LLMs when it comes to playing dice?
headline: Assessing LLM Accuracy in Dice Probability Tasks: A Benchmark Study
tldr: We benchmark eight state-of-the-art LLMs on standard and counterintuitive dice probability problems, testing with and without chain-of-thought prompting. Results show that while LLMs perform well on standard tasks, they struggle with counterintuitive scenarios, highlighting limitations in probabilistic reasoning.
significance: The study reveals critical gaps in LLMs' probabilistic reasoning, informing future model design and prompting strategies.

title: UniSHARP: Universal Sharp Monocular View Synthesis
headline: UniSHARP: Extending SHARP for Universal Monocular View Synthesis Across Camera Systems
tldr: UniSHARP generalizes the SHARP view synthesis framework to support a wide range of camera models, from perspective to fisheye and omnidirectional. By aligning images in a unified omnidirectional latent space, it enables photorealistic rendering across diverse viewpoints without pinhole-specific constraints.
significance: This work provides a versatile foundation for realistic monocular rendering in varied imaging contexts, paving the way for more flexible visual synthesis applications.

title: Agentopia: Long-Term Life Simulation and Learning in Agent Societies
headline: Agentopia: Simulating Long-Term Social Learning with LLM-Powered Agent Societies for Human Behavior
tldr: Agentopia explores whether large language models can learn from extended simulated social interactions, extending beyond the typical day-scale simulations. By modeling long-term agent societies, it aims to improve LLMs’ understanding of human behavior.
significance: It bridges the gap between short-term agent simulations and realistic human social learning, potentially enhancing AI’s social cognition.

title: MemDreamer: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Long Video Understanding via Hierarchical Graph Memory and Agentic Retrieval Mechanism
headline: MemDreamer: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Long Video Understanding via Hierarchical Memory
tldr: MemDreamer introduces a plug‑in framework that separates perception from reasoning, allowing incremental streaming of long videos into a hierarchical graph memory. An agentic retrieval mechanism then queries this memory, enabling efficient, scalable understanding without token explosion.
significance: This approach enables scalable, real‑time analysis of hours‑long videos, overcoming the limitations of current vision‑language models.

title: GJ 3929 b as the First Complete Rocky Worlds DDT Data Set
headline: GJ 3929 b: First Complete Rocky Worlds Dataset Reveals Atmosphere Search via JWST
tldr: The Rocky Worlds DDT survey has produced the first complete data set for GJ 3929 b, a low‑temperature rocky exoplanet orbiting an M dwarf. By probing day‑night heat redistribution and CO₂ absorption with JWST, the study seeks to determine whether such planets can sustain atmospheres.
significance: This dataset marks a pivotal step toward understanding atmospheric retention on rocky worlds around the most common stars in the galaxy.

title: Affordance-Based Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning for Quadruped Pedipulation
headline: Autonomous Quadruped Robots Learn Affordance-Based Hierarchical Control for Object Manipulation
tldr: This study presents a hierarchical reinforcement learning framework that enables quadruped robots to autonomously select affordable interaction points and base poses for object manipulation, eliminating the need for expert-designed trajectories. The approach demonstrates improved autonomy and efficiency in complex manipulation tasks.
significance: It advances autonomous manipulation in legged robots, reducing reliance on pre-programmed paths and enabling more flexible, real-world applications.

title: Differences in Detection: Explainability Where it Matters
headline: Comparing Object Detectors: DnD Highlights Explainability Gaps for Remote Sensing Applications
tldr: DnD provides a direct comparison of two detection models by evaluating the intersection of ground truth labels they both recognize, complementing mAP and TIDE metrics. This approach offers clearer insights into model strengths and weaknesses, aiding explainability in practical deployments.
significance: By revealing where models agree or diverge on actual detections, DnD enables more transparent model selection and targeted improvement.

title: Your UnEmbedding Matrix is Secretly a Feature Lens for Text Embeddings
headline: Revealing the Hidden Feature Lens: Why LLMs Fail as Off-the-Shelf Embedding Models
tldr: Large language models excel at zero-shot tasks but underperform as generic embedding providers. This study uncovers that their internal embedding space acts as a feature lens, explaining the mismatch and guiding future improvements.
significance: Understanding this hidden lens offers a path to enhance embedding quality for downstream applications, bridging the gap between LLMs and specialized embedding benchmarks.

title: Regret Minimization with Adaptive Opponents in Repeated Games
headline: Adaptive Opponents Challenge Traditional Regret Metrics in Repeated Game Learning
tldr: We propose Repeated Policy Regret (RP-Regret) to measure regret against adaptive opponents in repeated games. This metric captures counterfactual reasoning and overcomes limitations of external regret.
significance: RP-Regret provides a more accurate performance measure for online learning algorithms interacting with strategic, history-aware adversaries.

title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
headline: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution Enhances Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Large Language Models via Reinforcement Learning
tldr: RREDCoT introduces a segment-level reward redistribution technique that addresses delayed reward issues in chain-of-thought generation. By redistributing rewards across intermediate reasoning steps, the method improves model accuracy and training efficiency.
significance: This approach enables more effective RL fine-tuning of reasoning models, potentially boosting performance across a range of complex NLP tasks.

title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
headline: Self‑Augmenting Retrieval Boosts Diffusion Language Models
tldr: Discrete diffusion language models iteratively denoise entire responses, discarding low‑confidence tokens. These discarded tokens serve as a lookahead signal for retrieval‑augmented generation, improving output quality.
significance: It demonstrates a novel way to leverage diffusion model uncertainty for efficient retrieval‑augmented generation.

title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
headline: MLEvolve: Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Discovery Leveraging LLM Agents for Long-Horizon Optimization
tldr: MLEvolve introduces a self-evolving framework that enables LLM agents to autonomously discover and optimize machine learning algorithms over long horizons. By addressing inter-branch isolation, memoryless search, and hierarchical control gaps, it enhances sustained optimization in MLE tasks.
significance: This framework pushes the frontier of automated ML by enabling continuous, hierarchical self-optimization, potentially accelerating scientific discovery and engineering.

title: When positive and negative pairs differ in femtoscopy: residual Coulomb and isospin effects
headline: Charge-Dependent Femtoscopic Correlations Reveal Residual Coulomb and Isospin Dynamics in Heavy-Ion Collisions
tldr: We analyze identical-pion and kaon femtoscopic correlations, showing how the residual Coulomb field and isospin effects modify the correlation functions. The study uses a modified blast-wave model to propagate particles with opposite charges, revealing distinct charge-dependent signatures.
significance: This work clarifies how electromagnetic and isospin interactions shape femtoscopic observables, improving the interpretation of particle source sizes in high-energy collisions.

title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
headline: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning Enhances LLM Training Stability
tldr: We introduce a PC layer that reshapes weight matrices via low-degree polynomial preconditioning, improving conditioning during LLM training. After training, the weights can be merged back, incurring no inference overhead.
significance: It offers a simple, inference-free method to stabilize LLM training.

title: How abundant are good interpolators?
headline: Exploring the Prevalence of Effective Interpolating Linear Classifiers in High-Dimensional Data
tldr: This study quantifies how many unit‑norm linear separators perfectly fit labeled data under Gaussian mixture and logistic Gaussian feature models. It shows that even with negative margins, a surprisingly large fraction of interpolators achieve good generalization.
significance: Understanding the abundance of good interpolators informs why over‑parameterized models can still generalize well.

title: Goedel-Architect: Streamlining Formal Theorem Proving with Blueprint Generation and Refinement
headline: Goedel-Architect: Automating Lean 4 Theorem Proving via Blueprint Generation for Researchers
tldr: Goedel-Architect is an agentic framework that automates the creation and refinement of dependency graphs—blueprints—of definitions and lemmas in Lean 4. By leveraging large language models, it guides formal theorem proving, enabling rapid construction and iterative improvement of proofs.
significance: It demonstrates how foundation models can accelerate formal verification, reducing human effort in constructing rigorous proofs.

title: You Only Index Once: Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing
headline: Efficient Long-Context LLMs: Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing for Reasoning
tldr: This paper introduces a cross-layer sparse attention mechanism that shares routing decisions across layers, reducing decoding overhead for long-context LLMs. Experiments show comparable quality to dense attention while achieving significant speedups in reasoning-heavy inference.
significance: It enables scalable, high-quality inference for large language models without sacrificing accuracy.

title: Human Adults and LLMs as Scientists: Who Benefits from Active Exploration?
headline: Humans vs. LLMs: Active Exploration Boosts Conjunctive Causal Discovery
tldr: Adults struggle with conjunctive causal rules, while LLMs can improve through active exploration. The study compares human and LLM performance in causal learning tasks.
significance: Demonstrates that active exploration can mitigate human limitations in causal reasoning, informing AI-human collaboration.

title: Benchmark Everything Everywhere All at Once
headline: Revolutionizing Benchmarking: Sustainable, Scalable, and Reusable for LLMs and MLLMs
tldr: Benchmarks are essential for measuring LLM and MLLM performance but are costly to build and quickly become obsolete. This work proposes a framework for creating reusable, scalable benchmarks that maintain discriminative power over time.
significance: This framework addresses the sustainability and scalability bottlenecks in benchmark creation, enabling continuous progress in large language and multimodal models.

title: Flow-based Policy Adaptation without Policy Updates
headline: Flow-Based Policy Adaptation: Correcting Non-Expert Actions Without Updating Policies for Robotic Agents
tldr: We introduce GLOVES, a flow-based framework that transports suboptimal actions from pretrained policies or humans into expert-like behavior without retraining the underlying policy. This approach leverages existing knowledge while ensuring task-specific alignment and real-time applicability.
significance: GLOVES enables efficient deployment of pretrained agents by correcting noisy outputs on-the-fly, reducing the need for costly policy retraining.

title: TailLoR: Protecting Principal Components in Parameter-Efficient Continual Learning
headline: TailLoR: Low-Rank Updates to Protect Principal Components in Continual Learning
tldr: TailLoR uses the singular bases of pre-trained weights as a fixed reference to learn low-rank updates applied to the singular value matrix. A soft spectral penalty discourages updates aligned with dominant singular directions, preserving principal components during continual learning.
significance: By safeguarding dominant singular directions, TailLoR enables more stable and efficient parameter updates for continual learning of large foundation models.

title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
headline: Compact Command Interface for Humanoid Whole-Body Control via Distilled Teachers
tldr: We propose a compact, explicit command interface that bridges task planning and whole-body control for humanoid robots. Using distilled complementary teachers, the system reduces the need for dense kinematic references, enabling intuitive, modular control in real-time.
significance: This approach simplifies humanoid robot deployment by providing an intuitive, modular command space that eliminates the burden of dense kinematic references.

title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
headline: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Scalable Code Language Models Across Evolving Repositories
tldr: Code2LoRA introduces a hypernetwork that generates repository‑specific adapters, enabling code language models to incorporate local context without costly fine‑tuning. This approach scales to thousands of repositories and adapts to evolving codebases, improving import resolution and API understanding.
significance: By eliminating per‑repo fine‑tuning, Code2LoRA offers a practical, scalable solution for real‑world code understanding tasks.

title: The Missing Central Limit Theorems for Local Functionals of Berry's Random Wave Model
headline: Completing the CLT Landscape: Berry’s Random Wave Model Integrals in 2D and 3D
tldr: Researchers have established central limit theorems for integrals of third-degree Hermite polynomials of Berry's random wave model over expanding domains in two and three dimensions. This resolves the last missing pieces in the Wiener chaos framework for monochromatic random waves.
significance: The result finalizes the theoretical understanding of statistical fluctuations in monochromatic wave fields, enabling precise predictions for physical and engineering applications.

title: PAR3D: A Unified 3D-MLLM with Part-Aware Representation for Scene Understanding
headline: PAR3D: Unified 3D-MLLM with Part-Aware Representation Enhances Scene Understanding for Interaction
tldr: PAR3D introduces a part-aware representation into 3D multimodal large language models, enabling fine-grained understanding of scene geometry and object parts. This allows more accurate visual question answering, captioning, and referring segmentation, improving embodied interaction in 3D environments.
significance: By modeling part-level details, PAR3D bridges the gap between object-centric 3D-MLLMs and the nuanced requirements of real-world interaction, setting a new benchmark for scene understanding.

title: Phenomenology of Inflaton-Driven Early QCD Confinement and Solution to Axion Isocurvature Problem
headline: Inflaton-Driven QCD Confinement During Inflation Solves Axion Isocurvature Problem in the Early Universe
tldr: We show that coupling the inflaton to gluons raises the QCD confinement scale during inflation, making the axion heavy enough to suppress isocurvature perturbations. As inflation ends, the confinement scale drops, restoring a light axion consistent with cosmological constraints.
significance: This mechanism provides a natural solution to the axion isocurvature problem without fine-tuning the inflationary scale.

title: Statistically and Computationally Optimal Estimation and Inference of Common Subspaces
headline: Optimal Estimation of Shared Subspaces in Noisy Low-Rank Matrices
tldr: We analyze the fundamental statistical and computational limits for estimating a common subspace shared by multiple symmetric low-rank matrices corrupted by noise. The results delineate when accurate recovery is possible and when it is computationally hard, providing sharp thresholds for both regimes.
significance: This work establishes the precise boundaries between feasible and infeasible regimes for common subspace estimation, guiding both theory and practice in high-dimensional data analysis.

title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
headline: Benchmarking Progressive Human‑AI Co‑Editing for AI‑Text Detection
tldr: This study introduces a new benchmark that tracks how AI authorship signals evolve during iterative human‑AI editing. It reveals gaps in current detection methods that only analyze final documents.
significance: It provides the first systematic framework to understand and detect AI influence in collaboratively edited texts.

title: DNQ: Deep Nash Q-Network for Partially Observable n-Player Games
headline: DNQ: Deep Nash Q-Network Solves Multi-Player Bidding with Partial Observability
tldr: The paper introduces DNQ, a Deep Nash Q-Network that learns equilibrium strategies in partially observable, multi-player bidding games. By integrating a solver into the training loop, DNQ efficiently learns policies that approximate Nash equilibria in complex, simultaneous-bidding scenarios.
significance: DNQ advances multi-agent reinforcement learning by providing a scalable method to approximate Nash equilibria in realistic, partially observable competitive settings.

title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
headline: Supervised Memory Training Enables Efficient RNN Training Without Backpropagation Through Time
tldr: We introduce Supervised Memory Training (SMT), a novel approach that trains recurrent neural networks without relying on backpropagation through time. SMT eliminates sequential time dependencies, allows parallel computation, and mitigates vanishing/exploding gradients, enabling the learning of long-range dependencies.
significance: SMT offers a scalable, parallelizable alternative to BPTT, potentially transforming sequence modeling across domains.

title: Complexity-Balanced Diffusion Splitting
headline: Balancing Complexity in Diffusion Models: Introducing Complexity-Balanced Splitting for Efficient Generative Modeling
tldr: Standard diffusion models use a single large network across all timesteps, which is inefficient. CBS splits the model into complexity-balanced sub-networks, allocating capacity where needed and improving efficiency without sacrificing performance.
significance: CBS enables more efficient deployment of diffusion models by dynamically allocating computational resources, potentially reducing inference cost and enabling real-time applications.

title: Thinking with Imagination: Agentic Visual Spatial Reasoning with World Simulators
headline: Agentic Visual Spatial Reasoning: Using World Simulators to Infer Unobserved Layouts
tldr: Vision-language models excel at visual reasoning but struggle with spatial inference beyond observed views. This paper introduces agentic reasoning via world simulators to generate imagined layouts, ensuring cross-view consistency and robust reasoning from limited egocentric data.
significance: By enabling models to imagine unseen spatial configurations, the work advances AI's ability to reason about complex environments with sparse observations.

title: NASA Releases Galileo: The Open-Source Multimodal Model Advancing Earth ...
headline: NASA Unveils Galileo: Open-Source Multimodal Model for Earth Observation Data
tldr: NASA and partners release Galileo, an open-source foundation model that ingests diverse Earth observation data—optical, radar, elevation, climate, and maps—to enable scalable analysis. The model supports tasks from segmentation to time-series forecasting, aiming to democratize EO insights.
significance: Galileo democratizes advanced multimodal Earth observation analytics, enabling researchers and practitioners to rapidly develop applications across environmental monitoring, agriculture, and disaster response.

title: NASA Releases Galileo: The Open-Source Multimodal Model Advancing Earth ...
headline: NASA Unveils Galileo: Open-Source Multimodal Model Sets New Remote Sensing AI Standard
tldr: NASA has released Galileo, an open-source multimodal AI model that integrates satellite imagery, LiDAR, and SAR data for comprehensive Earth observation. Its multi-scale local-global learning and diverse pretraining enable advanced tasks like change detection, segmentation, and real-time monitoring.
significance: Galileo establishes a new benchmark for generalist remote sensing AI, empowering researchers and practitioners with versatile, high-performance tools.

title: GeoAI_Workshop_2025.ipynb - Colab
headline: Hands‑On GeoAI Workshop: Object Detection in Remote Sensing Imagery with Colab
tldr: This Colab notebook walks participants through installing GeoAI, downloading satellite imagery, visualizing data, training an object detection model, and performing inference. It provides a complete end‑to‑end pipeline for remote‑sensing object detection.
significance: It enables rapid prototyping of AI models for identifying objects in satellite imagery, accelerating research and practical applications.

title: GeoAI: Artificial Intelligence for Geospatial Data
headline: GeoAI: Bridging AI and Geospatial Analysis with a Powerful Python Toolkit
tldr: GeoAI is a Python package that integrates machine learning into geospatial workflows, enabling users to analyze satellite imagery, point clouds, and spatial data with ease. It offers intuitive APIs for tasks such as segmentation, object detection, and time‑series analysis, making advanced AI accessible to researchers and practitioners.
significance: By providing a unified, open‑source framework, GeoAI accelerates the adoption of AI in geospatial science, fostering innovation across environmental monitoring, urban planning, and disaster response.

title: Open Source Software - The Geological Remote Sensing Group (GRSG)
headline: GRSG Unites Free Geospatial Tools for Advanced Remote Sensing Across Optical, SAR, and Hyperspectral Data
tldr: The Geological Remote Sensing Group (GRSG) aggregates a suite of open‑source software and libraries for remote sensing tasks. It supports advanced image processing across optical, multispectral, SAR, and hyperspectral datasets, empowering researchers with free, powerful tools.
significance: By consolidating these resources, GRSG lowers barriers to entry, fostering innovation and collaboration in geospatial analysis worldwide.

title: Remote Sensing of Environment | Journal - ScienceDirect
headline: Remote Sensing of Environment Calls for Special Issue Proposals on Emerging Remote Sensing Technologies
tldr: The journal Remote Sensing of Environment is inviting researchers to submit special issue proposals focusing on new remote sensing missions and systems. Detailed submission guidelines can be found in the Journal Specific Information section of the Guide for Authors.
significance: This call encourages the advancement of remote sensing research by fostering focused thematic issues that highlight cutting‑edge missions and technologies.

title: International Society for Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
headline: International Society for Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing Drives Global Collaboration in Imaging Science
tldr: The International Society for Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing (ISPRS) is a non‑governmental organization that promotes international cooperation in the fields of photogrammetry and remote sensing. It supports research, education, and application development to advance imaging technologies worldwide.
significance: ISPRS plays a pivotal role in setting global standards and fostering innovation in imaging science, benefiting sectors from urban planning to environmental monitoring.

title: ICLR 2026 Papers
headline: Satellite Data Reveals Rapid Urban Expansion in Global Cities today
significance: This study demonstrates the power of high-resolution satellite imagery to track and quantify urban growth patterns, providing critical insights for sustainable city planning and climate change mitigation.

title: Top 10 Geospatial Analysis Software in 2025 - geowgs84.ai
headline: Top 10 Geospatial Analysis Software of 2025: AI, Remote Sensing, and Scalable Analytics
tldr: The 2025 landscape of geospatial analysis software showcases cutting‑edge AI, remote sensing, and scalable analytics tools tailored for urban planning, defense, environmental management, and automation. This list highlights open‑source and commercial solutions that excel in satellite imagery, LiDAR, SAR, and real‑time vector tile processing.
significance: These tools empower decision‑makers to leverage high‑resolution data and AI‑driven insights for rapid, data‑driven planning and risk mitigation.

title: Raster Vision: An open-source machine learning library for deep ...
headline: Raster Vision: Bridging Open‑Source Deep Learning and GIS
significance: Raster Vision democratizes advanced computer vision for geospatial analysis, enabling practitioners to build, benchmark, and deploy models on satellite and aerial imagery with minimal friction.

title: Thinking in Blender: Staged Executable Inverse Graphics with Vision-Language Models
headline: Vision‑Language Models Translate Single Images into Editable Blender Scripts for 3D Reconstruction
tldr: This paper shows that pretrained vision‑language models can generate Blender code from a single image, enabling executable inverse graphics without explicit 3D supervision. The resulting scripts allow direct rendering, relighting, and manipulation of the reconstructed scene.
significance: It demonstrates a new pathway to bridge 2D imagery and editable 3D scenes using foundation models, potentially accelerating content creation and interactive graphics.

title: New Windows on Heavy Dark Matter: Mineral Melt Modelling and X-Ray Readout for Muscovite Mica
headline: Muscovite Mica Reveals Heavy Dark Matter via Melt Tracks and X‑Ray Imaging
tldr: Scientists model melt track formation in muscovite mica and use X‑ray readout to detect heavy composite dark matter. This method leverages mica’s long‑term track retention to probe rare particle interactions.
significance: This approach provides a novel paleodetector for heavy dark matter, extending sensitivity beyond current experiments.

title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
headline: Reducing Perceptual Bias in Multimodal LLM Judges with Perturbation and Reward Modeling
tldr: Multimodal LLM judges often favor plausible narratives over perceptually correct answers when visual and textual cues conflict. This study introduces perceptual perturbation and reward modeling to mitigate this bias, improving evaluation reliability.
significance: By addressing perceptual judgment bias, the work enhances the trustworthiness of automated multimodal evaluation systems, crucial for high-stakes decision-making.
